body.title: sprint-ticket: proactive pre-brief before CC reads files
body.result: Implemented sprint_preflight_brief.py + updated sprint-ticket skill Step 2.3
body.intention: I intend that sprint-ticket assembles a proactive briefing from prior context before CC starts reading files, so relevant knowledge is surfaced without CC knowing to ask for it.
body.description:
Currently sprint-ticket calls recall(topic=<ticket title>) reactively — CC asks, Librarian answers. That misses things CC doesn't know to ask about. Anticipatory: when a ticket enters sprint, assemble a briefing from: (1) prior tickets that touched the same affected files, (2) decisions linked to this area, (3) any reset_count history + prior escalation summaries. Surface as a compact pre-brief before step 4 (plan review).

This is a push model, not pull. The briefing should be assembled by someone who has broader context than the current sprint prompt — Librarian, or a Granny dispatch step.

**Affected files:** ~/.claude/skills/sprint-ticket/SKILL.md; possibly devices/librarian/ if Librarian needs a new tool
**Design rules:** OOP-first if Librarian device change; log interface crossings at INFO
**Scope boundary:** Pre-brief step only — before plan review. Does not change build/test/commit steps. Token budget for briefing: cap at 500 tokens surfaced to CC.
**Completion criteria:** sprint-ticket has a pre-brief step between ticket claim and plan review. Pre-brief surfaces at least: prior tickets on same files, linked decisions. Token cost of briefing logged at INFO. Verified by running sprint on a ticket with known prior history and confirming the prior work appears.
